title: Aglaia iloilo
intro: The Aglaia iloilo, also known as the Iloilo aglaia or Aglaia, is a plant species belonging to the family Meliaceae, native to the Philippines and other parts of Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, due to its reported antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Aglaia iloilo include flavonoids, terpenoids, and limonoids, which have been isolated and studied for their potential therapeutic benefits. In traditional medicine, Aglaia iloilo is often used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Aglaia iloilo is a species of plant in the family Meliaceae. The genus Aglaia is a group of flowering plants found in tropical Asia, and it is closely related to the family of mahogany trees. The classification of Aglaia iloilo is further narrowed down to the subfamily Melioideae. This classification is based on the plant's morphology and genetic characteristics. Aglaia iloilo is also known to be a part of the order Sapindales, which includes other plants such as the soapberry and the guanabano.
uses: Aglaia iloilo is reported to have various medicinal uses, including its potential in treating fever and other illnesses. Traditionally, the plant is used in folk medicine to alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory issues. The bark and leaves of Aglaia iloilo are used to create medicinal preparations that help in reducing inflammation and relieving pain. In some regions, the plant is also used to treat wounds and infections due to its antimicrobial properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and its potential applications in modern medicine.
benefits: Aglaia iloilo is said to have various health benefits due to its rich source of bioactive compounds. The plant's extracts have been studied for their potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. It is believed to have anti-cancer properties and may help in reducing the risk of certain diseases. Aglaia iloilo's extracts have also been found to have neuroprotective effects, which may help in preventing neurodegenerative diseases such as Alzheimer's and Parkinson's. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential health benefits.
properties: Aglaia iloilo has been reported to possess various therapeutic properties, including potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. However, more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal benefits. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever and digestive issues. The extracts of Aglaia iloilo have been studied for their potential to inhibit the growth of certain microorganisms and to exhibit cytotoxic activity. Further investigation is necessary to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Aglaia iloilo for therapeutic purposes.
constituents: Aglaia iloilo contains a variety of active constituents, including lignans, flavonoids, and terpenoids. These compounds contribute to the plant's medicinal and pharmacological properties. Studies have isolated several lignans from Aglaia iloilo, including aglaiol, iliolin, and aglaiol-9'. The plant's flavonoids, such as flavone and flavanone derivatives, have been reported to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The terpenoids present in Aglaia iloilo may exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal properties.
